operatorDashboardSnapshot.summary: Sanitized view of the bot's real operator surfaces. Field names and thresholds are source-backed; live values are deliberately withheld.

panels.title: Dashboard bot status
panels.detail: Operator-facing status surface with bot liveness, uptime, heap usage, ping, and high-level runtime counts.
panels.scrub: Guild, user, and channel totals exist in the live payload but stay redacted here. nodeVersion and platform are also permission-gated.

panels.title: Public health check
panels.detail: Machine-readable health response for platform pings and deploy verification, including uptime and raw memory usage.
panels.scrub: The shape is real. Live timestamps, host platform details, and current heap values are intentionally omitted from the portfolio.

panels.title: Live update handshake
panels.detail: Handshake surface that advertises WebSocket availability, upgrade semantics, and connection count for live operator views.
panels.scrub: Current connection counts and deployment details are hidden, but the real route shape is preserved.

panels.title: Scheduler health snapshot
panels.detail: Loop health is derived from overall status, schedulerCount, heartbeat age, stale thresholds, and consecutive error state.
panels.scrub: Per-loop live ages and current errors remain redacted here, but the model mirrors the real health snapshot contract.

accessRules: nodeVersion and platform are stripped from bot status unless the caller has system-admin permission
public health and live-update routes stay machine-readable, but this portfolio removes current timestamps, live counts, and deployment identity
guild, user, and channel totals exist in the live dashboard payload and are intentionally omitted here

pressureProfile.summary: Build-time, source-derived runtime pressure profile. Snapshot-backed on purpose; not fake live telemetry.

metrics.label: Fastest loop
metrics.detail: rankings-auto-posting is the tightest scheduled cadence in the bot startup graph.

metrics.label: Fast loops
metrics.detail: 5 scheduler loops run at 1m-5m cadence and carry the highest heartbeat pressure.

metrics.label: Event-driven jobs
metrics.detail: 4 jobs stay dormant until work arrives instead of polling constantly.

metrics.label: Stale multiplier
metrics.detail: Heartbeat silence is judged against the configured stale window before a loop is treated as degraded.

metrics.label: Critical trip
metrics.detail: 13 scheduler paths explicitly wire errors into shared health state.

metrics.label: Cache namespaces
metrics.detail: 0 explicit memory/Redis namespaces keep cache boundaries visible instead of ad hoc.

notes: 4 public operator surfaces expose health/runtime status
5 recovery rails were detected across cache, analytics, scheduler health, and shutdown paths

recoveryRails.label: Scheduler stale downgrade
recoveryRails.detail: Heartbeat silence beyond the stale window marks loops degraded before they disappear entirely.

recoveryRails.label: Scheduler critical trip
recoveryRails.detail: Repeated scheduler errors promote the loop from degraded to critical instead of hiding the failure.

recoveryRails.label: Memory-only cache mode
recoveryRails.detail: If Redis is unavailable, the runtime stays up on memory cache instead of hard failing.

recoveryRails.label: Emergency cache cleanup
recoveryRails.detail: Heap pressure triggers cache flush and optional forced GC before the process spirals.

recoveryRails.label: Graceful shutdown path
recoveryRails.detail: Signals trigger structured cleanup instead of dropping the process cold.

incidentTimeline.title: Activity graphs stopped lying after restart and hydrate drift
incidentTimeline.summary: Two consecutive fixes repaired graph normalization and rebuilt the hourly buffer when restore paths lost its circular shape.
incidentTimeline.outcome: The dashboard keeps showing current-session pressure instead of flattening to zero after resets, warmup, or fallback paths.
incidentTimeline.logReceipt: [DASHBOARD] Falling back to legacy hourly metrics: {mongoError.message}

commits.summary: Fix analytics hourly activity buffer recovery
Fix dashboard analytics graph series normalization

incidentTimeline.title: Pickem lock boundaries were brought back into one consistent state
incidentTimeline.summary: The disabled-button check, persisted lock flag, and click-time game lookup were aligned so CBB picks stop acting closed while looking open.
incidentTimeline.outcome: Interaction state now matches what the poll communicates instead of splitting between UI and handler behavior.

commits.summary: Fix CBB pickem dead-button lock consistency and interaction lookup

incidentTimeline.title: Operator access and health visibility were hardened together
incidentTimeline.summary: Health endpoints, live update surfaces, rate limiting, and auth boundaries were tightened in the same layer instead of patched piecemeal.
incidentTimeline.outcome: The runtime stays inspectable to operators while dashboard access paths get stricter and more deliberate.

commits.summary: Implement full auth, config, health, and CI hardening pass
Harden auth and admin APIs; improve rate limiting and health checks

incidentTimeline.title: Memory containment moved from deploy folklore to designed rails
incidentTimeline.summary: Native rendering assumptions were simplified, obsolete allocator hacks were removed, and explicit cleanup paths remained in place for cache and shutdown pressure.
incidentTimeline.outcome: High-output periods rely on designed containment and recovery paths instead of hoping the process behaves.

commits.summary: fix(deploy): remove jemalloc — resvg subprocess model eliminates native memory fragmentation

recentOpsMoves.summary: Fix basketball score fallback and late tenths clock
Tighten image typography and grid theming
feat: update pickem and autoposter workflow
fix: clear AdvancedAnalytics singleton interval to prevent Jest hang
fix: set ENABLE_ANALYTICS=true in test env before module load
test: comprehensive dashboard analytics test coverage (101 tests)

receipts.label: Scheduler orchestration boot
receipts.signal: Centralized startup path and scheduler registration.

receipts.label: Health endpoint
receipts.signal: Operational runtime health surface for deploy/platform checks.

receipts.label: Heartbeat stale logic
receipts.signal: Per-loop stale detection and degraded/critical status calculation.

receipts.label: WebSocket runtime updates
receipts.signal: Dashboard update channel for live operator visibility.
